title: Grande Bleu Jeju Yacht: Honest Review of Korea's Catamaran
content:
Grande Bleu Jeju Yacht: An Honest Review of Korea's Only Certified Catamaran

Published: 2025 · Last updated: 2025 By Grande Bleu Yacht Crew · Verified Local Operator

Grande Bleu is Korea's only certified catamaran in Jeju, offering 60-minute sunset cruises from Daepo Port in Seogwipo.

If you live in Korea and you're plotting a summer escape that isn't another Bali or Phuket repeat, the Jeju yacht tour with Grande Bleu is one of those quietly underrated experiences that doesn't get talked about enough in English-language guides. Here's what an hour at sea actually looks like — without the marketing gloss.

Quick Facts

  • Location: Daepo Port, Seogwipo, Jeju Island (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E)
  • Duration: 60 minutes
  • Capacity: 91 guests across two catamarans (44 + 47)
  • Price: ₩60,000–₩70,000 adult / ₩40,000 child
  • Included: Unlimited drinks, snacks, ramyeon, fishing gear
  • Operating since: 2011 · 100,000+ guests served

Why a Catamaran Beats a Single-Hull (Especially in June–August)

Jeju summers (Jun–Aug) bring warm air, calm mornings and the kind of golden-hour light that flatters every photo. They also bring swell. The reason Grande Bleu Jeju is worth flagging over a standard sightseeing boat is the twin-hull design — two parallel hulls cutting the water instead of one, which means significantly less roll.

If you've ever sworn off boat trips because of seasickness, this is the design that changes your mind. It's also the only catamaran in Korea carrying full brand certification, which matters more than it sounds: a licensed captain and engineer are onboard every sailing, not just one or the other.

For couples doing Jeju sailing for the first time, this is the practical detail that separates a relaxing afternoon from a regrettable one.

FAQ

What is the cheapest Jeju yacht tour? The Luxury daytime tour at ₩60,000 per adult and ₩40,000 per child is Grande Bleu's most affordable option. Promotional rates can drop further on Klook. All food and drinks are included, so there are no on-board upsells.

How long is the Jeju sunset cruise? The cruise runs 60 minutes from Daepo Port, covering the Wolpyeong basalt columns, sea caves and Elephant Rock. The route ends with a 360-degree golden-hour panorama, available once daily at sunset.

Is food included on the Jeju yacht? Yes. Draft beer, red and white wine, Jeju tangerine juice, bottled water, local snacks and freshly cooked Korean ramyeon are all included with unlimited refills at no extra charge.

Where does the Jeju yacht depart from? Grande Bleu departs from Daepo Port in Seogwipo on Jeju Island's south coast (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E), roughly 50 minutes by car from Jeju International Airport.

Is the Jeju catamaran safe for kids and seniors? Yes. The twin-hull catamaran design minimises rolling motion, and every sailing carries both a licensed captain and engineer onboard. Grande Bleu has served over 100,000 guests since 2011 with partnerships with Shilla Hotel and Parnas Jeju.

What's the best time for a sunset cruise in Jeju? June to August offers the longest daylight and warmest sea breeze, making the late-afternoon Sunset Tour the prime slot. Book at least a week ahead for summer weekends.
